subject: the last night of a festival
lyrics:
[Verse]
Gates close at midnight, but the bass still breathes
Mud on my sneakers, glitter in my sleeves
Three days deep and we runnin' on fumes
Strangers turned brothers in the dust and the dunes
Last set loading, the DJ knows it
Every hand up like a candle, we hold it
Phone almost dead so I won't take a picture
Some moments you keep where the memory's richer

[Chorus]
One more song, one more song
We been gone all weekend, now the weekend's almost gone
One more song, sing it loud
We'll be ghosts in the morning, but tonight we are a crowd

[Verse]
Vendors packin' tents while the speakers still pumpin'
Girl on her brother's shoulders, the whole field jumpin'
I ain't got a number but I got a feeling
That the roof we don't have is the sky and the ceiling
Monday's gonna find me at a desk and a clock
But right now I'm forever, right now I don't stop

[Bridge]
Lights come up slow, security yawns
We walk to the parking lot trading our songs
Humming the hook like we'll never forget
We will, but not yet, no, not yet

[Chorus]
One more song, one more song
We been gone all weekend, now the weekend's almost gone
One more song, sing it loud
We'll be ghosts in the morning, but tonight we are a crowd

subject: driving past your old apartment at night
lyrics:
[Verse]
Las calles vacías, the streetlights all hum
I told myself this road was the long way home
But my hands knew the turns before my heart could come
And here is your window, and I am alone

[Chorus]
Ay, tu ventana sin luz
Dark as the day that you left me, mi amor
The trumpets still cry what my mouth never could
I drive past your door, but I don't stop no more

[Verse]
The jacaranda's grown over the gate where we'd meet
Somebody else hangs their wash on your line
The radio's playing our song down the street
And I curse every word, but I sing every line

[Bridge]
Guitarrón like a heartbeat too heavy to keep
The violins climb where my pride won't allow
I was too proud to call, now I'm too tired to sleep
So I drive, and I drive, and I leave you somehow

[Chorus]
Ay, tu ventana sin luz
Dark as the day that you left me, mi amor
The trumpets still cry what my mouth never could
I drive past your door, but I don't stop no more

subject: a poet who won't sell out
lyrics:
[Verse]
They came with their pockets all heavy and wide
Said, write us a jingle, we'll buy you the moon
But the clarinet laughed and the old man replied
My words are not bread you can sell by the spoon

[Chorus]
Oy, let the fiddle run free
Up the alley, down the stair, to the river and the sea
A verse can't be bought, a verse can't be sold
I'd rather have my freezing rhyme than someone else's gold

[Verse]
His coat had more patches than buttons or seams
His landlord kept knocking, his stove had gone cold
But he danced with the doynas, he feasted on dreams
And the truth in his notebook would never grow old

[Bridge]
The accordion sighs like a grandmother's prayer
The trumpet kicks up like a wedding gone wild
They offered him silver, he gave them his air
And walked off the stage like a stubborn, proud child

[Chorus]
Oy, let the fiddle run free
Up the alley, down the stair, to the river and the sea
A verse can't be bought, a verse can't be sold
I'd rather have my freezing rhyme than someone else's gold

subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
lyrics:
[Verse]
I carried the stone of your name for so long
Down to the riverside, heavy and slow
I rehearsed every word for the day you'd come home
But the day never came, and now you've gone, oh

[Chorus]
So I lay it down, lay it down by the grave
Every hard word I was saving I gave
I forgive you, I forgive you, though you cannot hear
Lord, you take it on to glory, I'll leave it right here

[Verse]
The choir is humming a low, gentle sound
The organ is breathing where bitterness lay
I thought I needed your hand to put the burden down
But grace doesn't wait for the debtor to pay

[Bridge]
Forgiveness ain't a letter that you mail to the dead
It's a window I'm opening inside of my chest
The wind takes the ashes of all that was said
And what's left of my heart can finally rest

[Chorus]
So I lay it down, lay it down by the grave
Every hard word I was saving I gave
I forgive you, I forgive you, though you cannot hear
Lord, you take it on to glory, I'll leave it right here

subject: an elder teaching the tide
lyrics:
[Verse]
He walks to the shoreline before the gulls wake
Salt in his beard, a map in his bones
He whispers the rhythm the water should take
And the sea, like a student, comes in over stones

[Chorus]
Come in, go out, the old man calls
Come in, go out, and the grey water falls
He taught it to breathe before the first lighthouse shone
And when he is gone, it will pull on alone

[Verse]
The drums start as footprints, then swell into surf
A single guitar like a line on the swell
He counts every wave from his birth to the earth
And the tide learns the patience it never could tell

[Bridge]
Louder, the strings, as the storm finds the bay
Louder, the kit, till the cymbals are foam
He stands in the breakers, his hair turning grey
Then the whole ocean quiets, and walks the man home

[Chorus]
Come in, go out, the old man calls
Come in, go out, and the grey water falls
He taught it to breathe before the first lighthouse shone
And when he is gone, it will pull on alone

subject: the night you almost made it
lyrics:
[Verse]
Velvet light low, the A&R came through
My band hit the pocket, the room felt brand new
I saw the whole future in the back of his eyes
Then he checked his phone twice and he straightened his tie

[Chorus]
So close, baby, so close I could taste it
One breath from the dream and I watched myself chase it
The night that I almost, almost broke through
I still hear that almost when the lights turn blue

[Verse]
He said, you got something, then he left through the door
Left a card on the table I still keep in my drawer
We played to the bartender stacking the chairs
Like the song was a promise that nobody hears

[Bridge]
Maybe almost is a place that you live
Maybe the want is the gift that you give
I didn't break, no, but I didn't break through
And I'm still here humming what almost came true

[Chorus]
So close, baby, so close I could taste it
One breath from the dream and I watched myself chase it
The night that I almost, almost broke through
I still hear that almost when the lights turn blue

subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
lyrics:
[Verse]
The morning shift bleeds into the night shift again
My coat smells of coffee and other men's floors
The bandoneón aches like a clock I can't win
And the rent stands in shadow outside of my door

[Chorus]
Dance with me, debt, you cruel partner of mine
You lead and I follow, two steps behind the line
I work and I work and the table stays bare
So I dance with the hunger, the only one there

[Verse]
My hands keep the rhythm of mop and of broom
Then I trade for an apron the second the sun dies
The violins climb up the walls of my room
While the numbers, the numbers, refuse to come right

[Bridge]
They say if you run you can catch up the rent
But the faster I spin, the more breathless I get
The music won't stop and my last coin is spent
And the floor's getting closer the lower I bet

[Chorus]
Dance with me, debt, you cruel partner of mine
You lead and I follow, two steps behind the line
I work and I work and the table stays bare
So I dance with the hunger, the only one there

subject: studying alone at two in the morning
lyrics:
[Verse]
Dos de la mañana, el café ya frío
Highlighter dry, but the page still mine
Everybody sleeping, this silence is mío
Just me and the textbook and the dembow in my mind

[Chorus]
Solo en la noche, dale, one more page
The city is dreaming but I'm on my grind
Dos de la mañana, I'm building my stage
Nobody watching, but I know what I'll find

[Verse]
Flashcards on the floor like the steps of a dance
Perreo with my pencil, I move to the beat
Mañana hay examen, this is my chance
The blueprint of tomorrow underneath my feet

[Bridge]
Mami called, said baby get some rest
But the future doesn't sleep, so neither do I
Every line that I learn is a coin in the chest
For the day that I walk with my name held high

[Chorus]
Solo en la noche, dale, one more page
The city is dreaming but I'm on my grind
Dos de la mañana, I'm building my stage
Nobody watching, but I know what I'll find

subject: the one that got away
lyrics:
[Verse]
There was a girl with a laugh like the dawn over hills
I met her one summer where the agave grows tall
I meant to say stay, but my pride had its will
And she boarded the bus, and I gave her no call

[Chorus]
La que se fue, the one that got away
The trumpets remember the words I won't say
I'd give all my silver, I'd give all my years
For one more mañana to dry up her tears

[Verse]
They tell me she married a man up in the north
That she sings to her children the songs that we knew
And I raise up my glass to the love I let forth
Like a dove from my hands into skies turning blue

[Bridge]
The guitarrón weeps where my courage once stood
The violins ask me the question I dread
Would you do it again, mijo, could you, you should
But the answer just rings in the wine and the dead

[Chorus]
La que se fue, the one that got away
The trumpets remember the words I won't say
I'd give all my silver, I'd give all my years
For one more mañana to dry up her tears

subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
lyrics:
[Verse]
Trunk full of mason jars rattling like teeth
Bald tires screaming down a county-line road
Sheriff's lights flooding the dark underneath
And Jimmy's behind me with a hell of a load

[Chorus]
Run it, run it, the still's gone dry
Run it, run it, kiss the law goodbye
We took the wrong turn at the broke-down sign
Now we're praying to a moon that don't shine

[Verse]
Hit the gravel too fast and the back end let go
Glass and white lightning all over the dirt
Jimmy went headfirst into a fence and a row
Of the corn that we stole just to cover the hurt

[Bridge]
Mama always told me this road ends in jail
Or a ditch or a casket or a story they tell
Well tonight it's a ditch and a busted-up tale
And the smell of the mash going straight back to hell

[Chorus]
Run it, run it, the still's gone dry
Run it, run it, kiss the law goodbye
We took the wrong turn at the broke-down sign
Now we're praying to a moon that don't shine

subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
lyrics:
[Verse]
A needle and thread in a small yellow room
The hum of the city pressed soft to the glass
He measures the morning, he hems the long gloom
And lets out the seams where the seasons have passed

[Chorus]
Every coat on the corner, he made it fit
Every sleeve in the window, he sat with it
The block wears his quiet, the block wears his care
A man you don't notice is everywhere there

[Verse]
The widow's black dress and the baby's first gown
The groom's nervous collar, the worker's torn knee
Soft as a tide, he stitches the town
Into something more whole than it knew it could be

[Bridge]
No song and no fanfare, just chalk and just pins
The slow turn of fabric, the patient repair
The world frays at evening, and quietly he begins
To gather the threads of the people back there

[Chorus]
Every coat on the corner, he made it fit
Every sleeve in the window, he sat with it
The block wears his quiet, the block wears his care
A man you don't notice is everywhere there

subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
lyrics:
[Verse]
The rooster ain't crowed yet, I'm already gone
Diesel and gravel and a thermos of black
Clock in at the plant till my shadow grows long
Then the diner till midnight, and I still fall back

[Chorus]
Two jobs, one grave, and the dirt's getting deep
I dig and I dig but I'm digging for keep
The banker's a crow on my windowsill
I work myself dead and I'm losing it still

[Verse]
My daddy did the same and they buried him broke
With calluses thick as the boards on his shed
I light the same cigarette, I cough the same smoke
And I wonder if living's just dying ahead

[Bridge]
The preacher says heaven don't cost you a dime
Well that's the one bill I might finally pay
But down here the meter just eats up my time
And the night comes to charge me for breathing all day

[Chorus]
Two jobs, one grave, and the dirt's getting deep
I dig and I dig but I'm digging for keep
The banker's a crow on my windowsill
I work myself dead and I'm losing it still

subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
lyrics:
[Verse]
The rain came down sudden on the cobblestone square
She ducked under my umbrella, I hadn't a chance
Two strangers, one circle of dry in the air
And the wet of the city turned into a dance

[Chorus]
Stay close, the rain won't forgive a wide space
Stay close, give the storm not an inch of its way
We don't know each other, but we share this one place
And the night holds its breath for the words we won't say

[Verse]
Her shoulder, my shoulder, the drum of the rain
A bandoneón breathing somewhere down the street
We walked half a block like a slow, secret chain
Two lives overlapping in the storm and the heat

[Bridge]
At the door of the station the downpour grew thin
She smiled and she vanished into the bright crowd
I still feel the warmth of her arm on my skin
And I'd buy back that storm if such things were allowed

[Chorus]
Stay close, the rain won't forgive a wide space
Stay close, give the storm not an inch of its way
We don't know each other, but we share this one place
And the night holds its breath for the words we won't say

subject: a chess hustler in the park
lyrics:
[Verse]
Five dollars a game underneath the grey trees
His clock slaps like rain on a window of fog
He hums to himself as he takes your last piece
A blur of a man in the wash and the smog

[Chorus]
Move, he says, move, and the world goes soft
The pawns drift like snow when the guitar lifts off
He's lost in the haze where the kings never fall
And nobody beats him at nothing at all

[Verse]
The tourists all flinch when he calls out checkmate
He pockets the bills like a tide pulling out
He's been here since morning, he'll stay here till late
A reverb of a smile and a half-hidden doubt

[Bridge]
Somewhere a life that he traded away
For pigeons and gambits and afternoons grey
The wall of sound rises, his hand finds the queen
And he disappears into all that's between

[Chorus]
Move, he says, move, and the world goes soft
The pawns drift like snow when the guitar lifts off
He's lost in the haze where the kings never fall
And nobody beats him at nothing at all

subject: the morning after a funeral
lyrics:
[Verse]
The casseroles cold in the back of the fridge
The black suit still hung on the back of the chair
I poured out two coffees from old, stubborn habit
Then stood with the second one, no one was there

[Chorus]
And the sun came up anyway, rude and so bright
The dog needed walking, the kettle still whined
The whole world kept turning like nothing was wrong
While I learned how to carry the you left behind

[Verse]
The phone keeps on buzzing with sorry and love
I nod at the window, I can't find my voice
The lawn needs a mow and the gutters are full
And grief is a morning with no other choice

[Bridge]
They say that it lessens, they say you go on
I guess that you do, 'cause here I still am
Doing the dishes, the dumb, holy dishes
Alive in the wreck of the after, god damn

[Chorus]
And the sun came up anyway, rude and so bright
The dog needed walking, the kettle still whined
The whole world kept turning like nothing was wrong
While I learned how to carry the you left behind
